title: Hi everyone!
author: Due_Term9335
selftext:
Hi everyone! 

I’ve recently started building an AI bot with Claude that trades on MT5  , it trades gold and 4 other instruments using a trend following strategy with 4-5 parameters, the parameters and inputs I believe are what I am struggling with most. 

Ive built it and i am at the backtesting step of the project and I just can’t seem to get it to a place where it’s 1. Profitable or 2. Looking good, no matter what I do it seems to just never be at a point that can actually work. 

I would love any information or any help on this, I’m not a very tech savvy person and this is my first time doing this ( enjoying it ) but it’s getting discouraging a little. 

Again would love any tips, tricks , advice or help on this, anything will help me at this stage, even different programs or sites i can use.

comments.author: BitsBoy96
comments.body: In general, AI can take you from level 1 to 5, but if you don't know how to write code to fix or add things, unfortunately, the AI ​​bot is difficult to operate in the long term.. because the market changes and you will have to adapt your bot as well.

comments.author: Alternative_Book_802
comments.body:
Backtest is a very good way to test your strategies. Which AI are u using? Are u trading yourself?   
I’m doing the same as u, just for crypto. I fed Claude with studies, lectures, videos, algos, etc.   
his knowledge is so insane now, that I mostly don’t even know if he’s right anymore.   
I have everything saved as files, so he knows about everything every session. Can send if u need

replies.author: Ai_copywriting_agent
replies.body: Can you send me them my Claude Kees forgetting

comments.author: surajmannn
comments.body: In reality I believe it’s quite hard to build strong AI without knowledge of AI models. 4-5 parameters is nowhere near enough to build strong AI. You need rich features which describe the learning task of the AI thoroughly. They also need to be distinguishable per your classification or predictable if regressive. Also depending what AI you’re using, but feature normalisation/scaling is very important. If you’re model can’t learn good/bad trade and market conditions from your parameters and data then it will basically be random
